name: Massachusetts
agency: Massachusetts Secretary of the Commonwealth, Corporations Division
address: One Ashburton Place, Room 1717, Boston, MA 02108-1512

steps: Vote to dissolve per operating agreement or majority vote of members
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File final federal and Massachusetts state tax returns
Close all tax accounts with the Massachusetts Department of Revenue
Obtain tax clearance from the Massachusetts Department of Revenue
File Certificate of Cancellation with the Corporations Division ($100 fee)
Cancel any DBAs, licenses, and permits
llc.notes: Massachusetts does not provide a standard form for LLC cancellation. The Certificate of Cancellation must be drafted to meet statutory requirements under MGL Chapter 156C. It must be signed by any manager or authorized person.

steps: Board of directors adopts a resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by two-thirds vote
File IRS Form 966 (Corporate Dissolution or Liquidation) with the IRS
Wind up business affairs, notify creditors, and settle debts
File final federal and Massachusetts state tax returns
Obtain tax clearance from the Massachusetts Department of Revenue
File Articles of Dissolution with the Corporations Division ($100 fee)
Must have filed all annual reports for the last 10 fiscal years
corporation.notes: A corporation must have filed all annual reports owed for the last ten fiscal years before the Corporations Division will accept the Articles of Dissolution. Tax clearance from the DOR is required.

tax.notes: Tax clearance from the DOR is required before dissolution. File final corporate excise tax returns and close all tax accounts. Corporations must notify the DOR of dissolution. All outstanding tax liabilities must be satisfied before clearance is issued.

timeline.waitingPeriods: Tax clearance process may take several weeks depending on filing history

requirements.employeeNotification: Follow federal WARN Act if 100+ employees; Massachusetts requires advance notice for plant closings
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors in writing; publish notice for unknown creditors
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Entity continues for purposes of winding up after dissolution filing
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLCs require majority vote of members unless operating agreement specifies otherwise. Corporations require board resolution and two-thirds shareholder approval.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
